definition: to make pain, a problem, or a difficult situation less severe

sentences.text: The new policy is intended to _____ pressure on small businesses.
Regular breaks can help _____ mental fatigue.
The treatment significantly _____ her symptoms.

definition: having more than one possible meaning and therefore unclear

sentences.text: The contract contains several _____ clauses.
His response was deliberately _____.
The instructions are too _____ to follow safely.

definition: logical, consistent, and easy to understand

sentences.text: She presented a clear and _____ argument.
The report lacks a _____ structure.
His explanation was detailed but not entirely _____.

definition: extremely convincing, interesting, or able to attract attention

sentences.text: The lawyer presented _____ evidence.
She gave a _____ reason for changing the plan.
The documentary tells a _____ story.

definition: to admit that something is true, often unwillingly

sentences.text: He eventually had to _____ that the criticism was valid.
The company _____ that errors had occurred.
She refused to _____ defeat.

definition: to think deeply and carefully about something

sentences.text: They are _____ a complete redesign of the system.
She paused to _____ the consequences.
He had never _____ leaving the company.

definition: to state the opposite of something or show that it is false

sentences.text: The new findings _____ the earlier report.
His actions clearly _____ his promises.
The witness later _____ her original statement.

definition: to become progressively worse

sentences.text: The situation may _____ if no action is taken.
The machine's condition _____ rapidly.
Relations between the two teams are _____.

definition: a difference between two things that should be the same

sentences.text: The auditor found a _____ in the financial records.
There is a clear _____ between the two measurements.
We need to explain several data _____.

definition: to explain or develop something in greater detail

sentences.text: Could you _____ on your proposal?
She _____ on the reasons behind the decision.
The report _____ the original concept in detail.

definition: to make a problem or bad situation worse

sentences.text: Poor planning may _____ the existing delays.
The shortage _____ tensions within the team.
Ignoring the issue will only _____ it.

definition: possible and practical to do successfully

sentences.text: We need to determine whether the proposal is _____.
The original schedule is no longer _____.
Engineers are studying several _____ alternatives.

definition: existing as a natural or permanent part of something

sentences.text: Every investment carries some _____ risk.
The process has several _____ limitations.
Uncertainty is _____ in long-term planning.

definition: showing extreme care and attention to detail

sentences.text: The engineer kept _____ records of every test.
Her _____ preparation prevented several mistakes.
The restoration required _____ work.

definition: no longer used because something newer or better exists

sentences.text: The old software became _____ after the update.
Many traditional devices are now _____.
The company replaced its _____ equipment.

definition: seeming reasonable or likely to be true

sentences.text: The investigator offered a _____ explanation.
That theory sounds _____ but lacks evidence.
We considered several _____ scenarios.

definition: common or widespread in a particular place or group

sentences.text: This problem is especially _____ in large cities.
Remote work has become increasingly _____.
The condition is more _____ among older adults.

definition: to make two opposing ideas, situations, or accounts consistent

sentences.text: It is difficult to _____ these conflicting reports.
The accountant _____ the two sets of figures.
She struggled to _____ her ambitions with her responsibilities.

definition: to examine something very carefully and critically

sentences.text: The committee will _____ every detail of the proposal.
The data was carefully _____ before publication.
Investors are _____ the company's latest results.

definition: to support a claim with evidence or proof

sentences.text: You must _____ your argument with reliable evidence.
The documents _____ her version of events.
The accusation could not be _____.

definition: not certain or final; subject to change

sentences.text: We have reached a _____ agreement.
The meeting date is still _____.
She gave a _____ answer because more data was needed.

definition: to weaken something gradually or secretly

sentences.text: Constant delays can _____ customer confidence.
His comments _____ the manager's authority.
Poor maintenance is _____ the system's reliability.

definition: never having happened or existed before

sentences.text: The company faced _____ demand last year.
The event caused _____ disruption.
Scientists recorded an _____ rise in temperature.

definition: capable of working successfully over time

sentences.text: We need a financially _____ solution.
The plan is technically possible but not commercially _____.
Only two options remain _____.

definition: to resist or survive a force, pressure, or difficult condition

sentences.text: The material can _____ extremely high temperatures.
The structure _____ the earthquake without major damage.
This device is designed to _____ heavy use.
